content.question: Whether the Hungarian 2013 KLIK re-centralization's failure to reduce inequality was due to centralization per se being ineffective, or due to its implementation coinciding with (and thus being masked by) simultaneous expansion of FBO schooling and demographic decline in rural areas.
content.reduction_path: Decomposition analysis of Hungarian achievement inequality (Gini coefficient or variance decomposition) over the pre/post-2013 period, with controls for regional FBO density, settlement-level population change, and change in teacher qualification/experience distribution, to isolate the independent effect of governance structure from concurrent sectoral shifts.
